title: rubus lejeunei
intro: Rubus lejeunei, also known as Lejeune's black raspberry, is a type of bramble native to the southeastern United States. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions, and are also believed to have antiviral and antibacterial properties. Rubus lejeunei can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, with the leaves and fruits being used in these preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
